title: QA followups (S90/S96 verification) + entity create/delete probes + input inject + game-design dogfood

stories.title: POST /__agf/entity creates a new entity with an initial component map
stories.summary: runtime.createEntity(entityId, components) calls world.addEntity + iterative world.setComponent for each [name, value] pair; returns { kind: 'ok', components } on success or { kind: 'entity-exists' } when the id is taken. The dev-bridge POST /entity route runs the new exported pure helper `validateEntityCreateBody` (rejects non-object body, empty/non-string entityId, missing/non-object/array components with 400 AGF_BRIDGE_INVALID_ENTITY_CREATE), forwards via the new page-bridge `entity-create` RPC, and maps `entity-exists` to 409 AGF_PROBE_ENTITY_EXISTS. Plumbed end-to-end through src/app.ts + window.__agf.createEntity.
verification: acceptance: POST /__agf/entity { entityId: 'qa-probe-1', components: { Foo: { x: 1 } } } returns 200; a subsequent GET /__agf/entity/qa-probe-1 returns the same components. Re-posting the same id returns 409 AGF_PROBE_ENTITY_EXISTS.
engine/runtime/start.ts: createEntity helper on the runtime handle + interface entry
engine/dev/agf-dev-bridge.ts: exported validateEntityCreateBody + POST /entity route mapping entity-exists → 409

+8 unit tests in tests/unit/entity-create-bridge.test.ts: canonical shape, empty components, non-object body (null/string/number/undefined), missing entityId, empty/non-string entityId, missing/non-object components, array-as-components rejection

stories.title: DELETE /__agf/entity/<entityId> removes an entity wholesale
stories.summary: Completes the entity CRUD triplet (create / read / delete + S097's component write). runtime.deleteEntity(entityId) calls world.removeEntity and returns { kind: 'ok' } | { kind: 'entity-not-found' }. The dev-bridge DELETE /entity/:entityId route reuses parseEntityPath (S097), forwards via the new page-bridge `entity-delete` RPC, maps entity-not-found to 404. Required widening the method gate from {GET, POST} to {GET, POST, DELETE}. Plumbed through src/app.ts + window.__agf.deleteEntity.
verification: acceptance: After POST /__agf/entity { entityId: 'test.1', components: {} }, DELETE /__agf/entity/test.1 returns 200 with { deleted: 'test.1' }; a subsequent GET /__agf/entity/test.1 returns 404 AGF_PROBE_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND.
engine/runtime/start.ts: deleteEntity + interface entry
engine/dev/agf-dev-bridge.ts: DELETE /entity/:entityId reuses parseEntityPath; method gate widened to include DELETE
Reuses the parseEntityPath unit tests from S097 (entity-at-bridge.test.ts) — same path parser, no new path tests needed

stories.title: POST /__agf/input/action lets QA fire game inputs without DOM focus (QA-006)
stories.summary: Promoted from QA-2026-05-20-006. Project-agnostic input-injection surface: engine ships a generic `InputAction { action, value? }` transient component (schema in schemas/components/gamefeel.schema.json, referenced from scene.schema.json). runtime.injectInput(entityId, action, value?) writes the transient + returns { kind: 'ok' | 'entity-not-found' }. Project input systems read + clear it each frame, same way they translate keyboard events into project-specific transients. examples/kaboom-crew/src/systems/player-input-system.ts grows the consumer path: 'place-bomb' → PlaceBombRequest, 'restart' → RoundRestartRequest, 'move-up/down/left/right' → GridMover.queuedDirection vector, 'stop' → {0,0}. Unknown action names are silently consumed (engine remains project-agnostic; projects can grow new verbs without engine changes). Dev-bridge POST /input/action runs the new exported `validateInputInjectBody`; missing/empty entityId or action returns 400 AGF_BRIDGE_INVALID_INPUT_ACTION; entity-not-found maps to 404.
verification: acceptance: POST /__agf/input/action { entityId: 'player.1', action: 'place-bomb' } triggers the same downstream effect as pressing Space — a Bomb entity spawns on the player's cell on the next fixedUpdate. Verified in unit test 'InputAction place-bomb fires PlaceBombRequest the same frame and removes itself'.
engine/runtime/start.ts: runtime.injectInput tagged-union return; InputAction component schema in gamefeel.schema.json
engine/dev/agf-dev-bridge.ts: exported validateInputInjectBody + POST /input/action route
examples/kaboom-crew/src/systems/player-input-system.ts: 7-verb consumer (place-bomb / restart / move-{up,down,left,right} / stop) + safe no-op for unknown verbs

+6 unit tests on validateInputInjectBody (canonical shape, optional value, non-object body, missing entityId/action, arbitrary action string acceptance) +5 on the Kaboom Crew adoption (place-bomb, move-right, all 5 cardinals, restart, unknown-action no-op)

stories.title: engine-cli 'prints a stable inspect summary' test flakes at 5000ms (QA-004)
stories.summary: Promoted from QA-2026-05-20-004. Each `it()` in tests/unit/engine-cli.test.ts spawns a fresh `tsx engine/tools/cli.ts` — cold-start of tsx + the full CLI dep graph reliably blows the 5000ms vitest default on slower workers. Fix: per-test timeout override `CLI_TEST_TIMEOUT_MS = 15000` applied to every CLI integration case. Pre-warm was considered but rejected — each `spawn` is a fresh process so Node's module cache doesn't survive across spawns; a beforeAll warmup costs cycles without speeding the individual cases. Test-infra fix only; no product change.
verification: acceptance: `npx vitest run tests/unit/engine-cli.test.ts` is green; observed duration ~3s warm / ~7s cold for the 4 original cases plus the new 3x regression. No timeout failures across 5 consecutive runs.
tests/unit/engine-cli.test.ts: every `it()` in the engine-CLI describe gets CLI_TEST_TIMEOUT_MS = 15000 as its per-test timeout

stories.title: Lock the engine-cli flake fix with a deterministic timing test (QA-005)
stories.summary: Promoted from QA-2026-05-20-005 (regressionFor QA-004). Adds 'S98 regression: 3x warm inspect runs finish under 10s each' — loops 3 inspect runs back-to-back, measures wall-clock per run, asserts each < 10000ms. Per-test timeout is CLI_TEST_TIMEOUT_MS * 3 = 45000ms to cover the full sequence. Catches future regressions on either axis: a stuck process (>10s wall) or a slow-but-finishing process (still <10s each but cumulative >45s).
verification: acceptance: The 3x regression case is green on current main; would fail on the pre-S98 5000ms-default codebase (3 sequential cold runs blow 5s easily).
tests/unit/engine-cli.test.ts: 'S98 regression: 3x warm inspect runs finish under 10s each'

stories.title: First end-to-end dogfood of the propose:new → propose:promote flow
stories.summary: Exercise the S097-shipped game-design agent flow with real proposals. (1) `npm run propose:new -- "Decal-on-grid renderer primitive (M27)" --kind feature --priority should --epic M27-DECAL-LAYER` + fill in intent/rationale/acceptanceHints from notes/dynabomber-readiness-analysis.md §14. (2) `npm run propose:new -- "Region-rule modifier primitive (M28)" --kind mechanic --priority could --epic M28-REGION-RULES` + fill in. (3) Verify both pass `npm run backlog:check`. (4) Create a pending S099 sprint shell so `npm run propose:promote -- --into S099 --skip-check` has a target. (5) Promote both proposals. (6) Verify the S099 sprint JSON now has two pending stories with FEAT- prefixes and the source files moved under archive/S099/.
verification: acceptance: At the end of this story, S099.sprint.json (pending) exists with at least 2 pending FEAT-* stories that came from the two GDP proposals; backlog/proposed-stories/archive/S099/ contains the source files; backlog/proposed-stories/ (current) is empty again.
Captures any gaps in the S097 tooling that the dogfood reveals (e.g. unclear error messages) as inline comments in the sprint summary so a follow-up story can address them.

stories.title: docs/qa/invalid-ticket-handling.md — how dev rejects QA tickets that don't apply
stories.summary: Define the convention captured ad-hoc in this sprint (rejecting QA-001 + QA-003). When dev decides a QA ticket should NOT promote into a sprint (already-fixed, intentional behavior, duplicate, etc.), they `git mv` the JSON file under `backlog/qa-tickets/archive/rejected/` and append a one-line rationale to that directory's README.md. QA can still see the ticket; the directory tells them dev considered it and declined. Documents both halves: when dev rejects (and why), and what QA does when they see a ticket get rejected (don't refile; argue in a comment if needed).
verification: acceptance: docs/qa/invalid-ticket-handling.md exists and answers: 'when does dev reject a ticket?', 'where does it land?', 'what does QA do next?'. Cross-links to docs/qa/design.md and docs/qa/agent.md.
The README.md inside backlog/qa-tickets/archive/rejected/ (already created this sprint) gets a link to the new doc.

stories.title: Danger overlay on cells inside a bomb's projected blast radius
stories.summary: Visual readability polish: while a bomb's fuse is < 1 s remaining, paint a translucent red overlay on every cell that the eventual blast will cover. Distinct from the post-detonation BlastTile (which already exists) — this is the PREDICTION layer so the player can dodge in time. Uses the existing `projectedBlastCells(world, gx, gz, range)` helper from examples/kaboom-crew/src/danger.ts. Tile component: project-local DangerTile entity (color: '#ff3a3a', alpha: 0.35, layer: 'fx-below') auto-removed when the bomb detonates or its fuse > 1 s again. Lays groundwork for the M27 decal layer epic without requiring it.
verification: acceptance: As any bomb's fuse drops below 1 s, every cell it will eventually scorch lights up red until detonation; the red disappears cleanly when the blast resolves.
examples/kaboom-crew/src/systems/danger-decal-system.ts: walks Bomb entities with fuseRemaining < 1 s; reuses projectedBlastCells; spawns/removes per-cell DangerTile entities
+ unit tests: a bomb with fuseRemaining=0.5 spawns DangerTiles for every projected cell; fuseRemaining=2 spawns none; removing the bomb removes its DangerTiles
